heading: Letters patent
label: 497 Letters patent
text: Whoever falsely makes, forges, counterfeits, or alters any letters patent granted or purporting to have been granted by the President of the United States; orWhoever passes, utters, or publishes, or attempts to pass, utter, or publish as genuine, any such letters patent, knowing the same to be forged, counterfeited or falsely altered—Shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than ten years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 71 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 2735 Stat. 1094Mandatory punishment provision was rephrased in the alternative.Changes were made in phraseology.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $5,000”.Pub. L. 103–322
